data.title: Royalty Pharma in $500M R&D funding collaboration with J&J
data.description:
• Royalty Pharma (RPRX) is partnering with Johnson & Johnson (JNJ) in a $500M R&D funding deal over 2026–2027 to support the development of a new autoimmune treatment.
  • JNJ‑4804 is a novel co‑antibody therapy that blocks the complementary interleukin‑23 (IL‑23) and tumor necrosis factor (TNF) pathways, delivering synergistic effects on the pathogenesis of chronic immune‑mediated diseases.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Oil surges as Brent nears record monthly gain following Houthi attacks on Israel
data.description:
Oil prices continued to climb on Monday, with Brent (CO1:COM) nearing a record monthly gain, after weekend attacks by Yemen’s Houthis on Israel intensified the broader Middle East conflict involving the U.S., Israel, and Iran.

Brent crude futures (CO1:COM) jumped 2.4% to $115.26 a barrel after settling 4.2% ‌higher on Friday. The benchmark was headed for a ~60% monthly gain. U.S. West Texas Intermediate (CL1:COM) was up 1.8% at $101.45 after a 5.5% gain in the previous session, up 51% this month.

Tight physical market conditions are also evident in the forward curve, with Brent’s prompt spread in deep backwardation, ING analysts said. "The front‑month contract traded at a premium of more than $7/bbl on Monday, compared with a largely flat structure before the conflict, underscoring acute near‑term supply concerns."

Elsewhere, positioning data shows speculative interest remains elevated despite some trimming. Speculators reduced their net long in ICE Brent by 21,579 lots to 407,125 lots as of last Tuesday, driven largely by a reduction in gross longs, while positioning changes in NYMEX WTI were modest. Ongoing geopolitical uncertainty and supply risks have continued to underpin risk‑on positioning in the oil market so far this year, the analysts said.

European natural gas prices also moved sharply higher, rising as much as 3.4%, as the escalation in the Middle East reinforced concerns over LNG availability ahead of the summer storage refill season.

The Israeli military said on Monday that it was attacking the Iranian government's infrastructure ⁠throughout Tehran.

data.title: Veradermics reports Q4 results
data.description:
• Veradermics, Incorporated press release (MANE): Q4 Cash, cash equivalents, and marketable securities totaled $141.9 million as of December 31, 2025. 
  • Net loss was $21.8 million for the fourth quarter of 2025, compared to a net loss of $5.7 million for the fourth quarter of 2024.
  • Veradermics expects its current cash, cash equivalents, and marketable securities to support its current operating plans into 2029.

data.title: Seagate initiated with Overweight rating at JPMorgan on cloud demand, pricing strength
data.description:
Seagate Technology (STX) was initiated with an Overweight rating and a $525 price target at JPMorgan, as the investment firm cited demand from cloud computing and pricing strength.

“We are initiating coverage on shares of Seagate with an Overweight rating, despite the 350% rise in the share price from early 2025 (vs. 11% for S&P 500) on the opportunity for significant upside to consensus estimates, with the [hard disk drive] industry positioned to benefit from both strong demand stemming from hyperscaler capex plans as well as pricing tailwinds,” analyst Samik Chatterjee wrote in a note to clients. “We forecast a 25% revenue CAGR and a 50%+ operating earnings CAGR over the medium-term, which we believe justifies the re-rating of STX shares and supports our Dec-26 price target of $525, implying 39% upside potential.”

Delving deeper, Chatterjee said the pace of cloud computing capital spending and the artificial intelligence infrastructure buildout has been “robust” and is likely to be for some time. 

“The HDD industry and Seagate are positioned to see robust demand growth fueled by cloud capex growth in the 50%+ range medium-term,” Chatterjee added. “Storage exabyte growth is forecasted to expand in the mid-20% range medium-term, and the strong demand cycle should support a better pricing environment than the industry and Seagate have experienced in the past, wherein cost/technology improvements can be leveraged for positive outcomes on financial metrics.”

Lastly, given that the hard disk drive market is essentially an oligopoly with Seagate and Western Digital (WDC), pricing is likely to be a tailwind for some time, Chatterjee said, as both have committed “to remaining disciplined.”

“This discipline should support pricing tailwinds and expansion in gross profit per exabyte over the medium-term, while the faster-than-expected transition to HAMR, wherein Seagate just qualified with a second customer for Mozaic 4, should support upside in relation to exabyte growth,” Chatterjee added.

data.title: Infographic: Sysco enters high-margin cash & carry segment via landmark $29.1B acquisition
ALT5 Sigma files to delay 10-K
data.description:
Alt5 Sigma (ALTS) said it is unable to file its annual report for the fiscal year ended December 27, 2025, on time without incurring unreasonable effort or expense.

The company said it needs additional time to finalize its annual financial statements, citing accounting treatment related to a recent acquisition and delays in completing the audit following a change in its PCAOB-registered accounting firm.

data.title: Coca-Cola expands Fairlife production capacity amid strong demand
data.description:
Coca-Cola (KO) is expanding Fairlife with a major investment in Michigan. The beverage giant is committing up to $650M to add two new production lines at its Coopersville plant, expand the facility by about 245K square feet, and create roughly 150 jobs. 

The new Fairlife lines are expected to begin commercial production in 2028, and the company is also planning to open a separate Fairlife facility in Webster, New York, later this year. The expansion appears aimed at easing a capacity crunch as Fairlife continues to grow, especially in lactose-free milk and protein shake products. Notably, Fairlife has become a key component of Coca-Cola's (KO) growth strategy as it pushes further into dairy and protein beverages beyond soda.

Coca-Cola's (KO) relationship with Fairlife dates back to 2012, when it acquired a minority stake and entered a national distribution partnership with Select Milk Producers around the brand’s early development and growth. Fairlife was built around ultra-filtered dairy and initially centered on Core Power, a high-protein milkshake that helped establish the brand’s position in value-added dairy. In January 2020, Coca-Cola (KO) bought the remaining stake in Fairlife from Select Milk Producers and became the company’s sole owner. After that, Fairlife continued to operate as a standalone business.

Coca-Cola (KO) treats Fairlife as a key part of its "better-for-you" beverage strategy, expanding the brand into protein shakes, creamers, and ultra-filtered milk. During the company's last earnings conference call, management pointed to momentum with the Fairlife business.

data.title: Compass Diversified to sell food service unit to Archer for enterprise value of ~$292M, shares up
data.description:
Compass Diversified (CODI) was trading higher after the middle-market businesses operator announced a sale of the food service unit of its majority-owned subsidiary, SternoCandleLamp Holdings.

CODI entered into a definitive agreement to sell the unit to Archer Foodservice Partners for an enterprise value of $292.5M.

Shares were 4.79% higher to $7.00 before the market open on Monday.

Net proceeds will be used to repay outstanding debt.

In 2025, the unit generated subsidiary adjusted EBITDA of ~$30.3M.

On account of the transaction, the company expects its senior secured net leverage ratio to fall below 1.0x, and it expects to avoid fees associated with excess leverage under its senior secured indebtedness beyond June 30.

The transaction is expected to close in May 2026.

The company will retain Sterno's home fragrance business.

data.title: Americas Gold and Silver Non-GAAP EPS of -$0.13 misses by $0.04; gives 2026 production outlook
data.description:
• Americas Gold and Silver press release (USAS): FY Non-GAAP EPS of -$0.13 misses by $0.04.
  • Adjusted EBITDA1 for 2025 was a loss of $4.1 million ($0.02 per share) compared to adjusted EBITDA loss of $1.5 million or ($0.01 per share) for 2024.
  • Consolidated silver production of approximately 2.65 million ounces during 2025 was higher than 2024 production of approximately attributable 1.7 million ounces.
  • Cost of sales1,2 per silver equivalent ounce produced, cash costs1, and all-in sustaining costs1 per silver ounce produced averaged $24.98, $25.69, and $32.95, respectively, in 2025.
  • Consolidated attributable silver production increased 52% year-over-year.
  • Consolidated 2026 production and cost guidance of 3.2 to 3.6 million ounces of silver at an average AISC1 of $30 to $35 per ounce sold.

data.title: Aura Biosciences GAAP EPS of -$1.76 beats by $0.19
data.description:
• Aura Biosciences press release (AURA): FY GAAP EPS of -$1.76 beats by $0.19.
  • As of December 31, 2025, the company had cash and cash equivalents and marketable securities totaling $144.2 million.
  • The company believes its current cash and cash equivalents and marketable securities are sufficient to fund its operations into the first quarter of 2027.
